revision_history.summary: Critic round-1 revisions: explicit unit-frame discipline + RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION constant (C-1 blocker); corrected codebase-identity line citations to autotuner.py:672-695 / :752-777 (C-2 blocker); IEEE-754 floor-sizing arithmetic corrected; closed_form_check block added (S-5); W-H4-B information-collapse residual disclosed; Carver/Chan citation softened to qualitative-directional (S-6); persistent_NN1_facet clarified as the form, not the data source (S-3).

selected_wealth_argument_formula.name: per_period_gross_wealth_ratio
selected_wealth_argument_formula.pseudo_code_decimal_fraction_frame: W_i = max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, 1 + r_i_policy_fraction)  where  r_i_policy_fraction = triggered_return_i_pct / RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION  (equivalently (guard_alpha_i_pct + eod_return_i_pct) / RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION)
selected_wealth_argument_formula.unit_frame_of_formula: decimal-fraction (W = 1.05 for +5% day; W = 0.99 for -1% day)

unit_conversion_constant.name: RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION
unit_conversion_constant.applied_at_boundary: autotuner branch: between _collect_sim_returns (emits percent-unit series) and compute_crra_eu_objective (consumes decimal-fraction series)

selected_wealth_argument_formula.codebase_identity_source: autotuner.py:672-695 (lines 673, 688, 692) and autotuner.py:752-777 (lines 753, 769, 777); r_policy === triggered_return; algebraic rearrangement triggered_return === guard_alpha + eod_return
selected_wealth_argument_formula.domain: W in (0, +inf); after floor W in [WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, +inf)
selected_wealth_argument_formula.codomain_of_U: U in (u(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR; gamma), +inf); always finite for gamma in [0, 10]
selected_wealth_argument_formula.floor_target: INPUT W (NEVER output U); flooring U inflates t-stat (anti-conservative)
selected_wealth_argument_formula.rf_adjustment: NONE; r_f = 0 by MANDATE (operator decision PA-5, matches SORTINO_TARGET_RETURN=0.0 at autotuner.py:117-118)
selected_wealth_argument_formula.aggregation: one U_i per fold-day; mean(U) and sd(U) computed over T fold-days within one Optuna trial
selected_wealth_argument_formula.scope: M1 autotuner branch only (spec_bundle.objective_kind == 'crra_eu'); Sortino branch untouched
selected_wealth_argument_formula.persistent_NN1_facet: The DEFINITIONAL FORM W = max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, 1 + r^policy) is the frozen NN1 facet. Phase-1 r^policy is the realized per-day return on the actually-triggered exit. Phase-2 (if Finalist B unlocks) hold-branch r^policy is the simulated per-day return on the no-exit counterfactual from simulate_forward_paths. The form persists across phases; only the data source changes. A-4 consistency holds at the FORM level.

crra_utility_formula.general: u(W; gamma) = (W^(1 - gamma) - 1) / (1 - gamma)   for gamma != 1
crra_utility_formula.general_form_pin: The numerator MUST include the '-1' term. Some texts state CRRA without it (u(W) = W^(1-gamma)/(1-gamma)); both forms are monotone-equivalent for argmax purposes (differ by a gamma-dependent constant -1/(1-gamma)) but NOT mean-equivalent. compute_crra_eu_objective consumes mean(U), and the haircut t-stat consumes (mean(U), sd(U)); sd(U) is invariant under a constant shift (the '-1' drops), but mean(U) is NOT — feeding the unsubtracted form would shift mean(U) by -1/(1-gamma), changing both the reported CE displayed value and the t-stat's NUMERATOR. The fixture's worked U values are computed with the '-1' term; the M1 implementer MUST match this form exactly.
crra_utility_formula.log_branch_form_pin: ln(W) without offset; the gamma->1 limit of (W^(1-gamma) - 1)/(1-gamma) is ln(W) by L'Hopital, so the '-1' subtraction is implicit in the log branch and no further offset is added.
crra_utility_formula.log_branch_provenance: Merton 1969 / Samuelson 1969 derive log utility as the gamma->1 limit; the tolerance avoids 1/(1-gamma) blow-up near gamma=1.

closed_form_check.purpose: Pin the EXACT functional form the SUT must match; prevent silent drift to a monotone-equivalent variant.
closed_form_check.rejected_variant_no_minus_1: u = W**(1.0 - gamma) / (1.0 - gamma)   -- monotone-equivalent for argmax; NOT mean-equivalent; REJECTED
closed_form_check.rejected_variant_alt_offset: u = (W**(1.0 - gamma) - W0**(1.0 - gamma)) / (1.0 - gamma)  for any W0 != 1  -- changes mean(U); REJECTED unless W0=1 (which reduces to the canonical form)

justification_legs.leg: THEORY (IEEE-754 stability — wide tolerance, not the binding leg)
justification_legs.claim: At W=1e-3 and prudential gamma=5, W^(1-gamma)=W^(-4)=1e12, leaving ~296 orders of magnitude of slack against IEEE-754 double's 1.8e308 ceiling. Even at the Mehra-Prescott implausible-prudential cap gamma=10, slack remains ~282 orders of magnitude. The IEEE-754 envelope is not the binding constraint; any floor in [1e-4, 1e-2] is numerically safe at prudential gamma<=10.

A realized per-day return r <= -99.9% implies the protective stop has catastrophically failed; in-distribution Planet Stopper trailing-stop-protected long-only US-equity strategies do not see daily losses approaching -99% (Carver 2015, Systematic Trading; Chan 2013, Algorithmic Trading establish the qualitative claim; the specific empirical percentile thresholds are not pinpointed because the directional claim — -99% is not in-distribution — is what is load-bearing). The floor at 1e-3 sits well below any realistic in-distribution loss day; its purpose is numerical correctness in replay/backtest contexts with pathological synthetic inputs.
.claude/CLAUDE.md 'Coding Standards (project additions)' requires named module-scope constant with source comment; this fixture is the source citation.

WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR.applied_to_negation: NEVER to output U (would compress lower tail, shrink sd(U), inflate t-stat -- evaluation A.1 H-1 binding)

rejected_candidates.name: guard_alpha_as_W
rejected_candidates.reason: guard_alpha is a signed difference, not a wealth ratio; CRRA domain requires W>0; this is the W-H2 defect the derivation closes.

rejected_candidates.name: net_of_rf_W = 1 + (r_policy - r_f)
rejected_candidates.reason: Inconsistent with operator MANDATE (SORTINO_TARGET_RETURN=0); introduces benchmark drift between Sortino branch and CRRA branch (A-4 violation).

rejected_candidates.name: log_return_W = ln(1 + r)
rejected_candidates.reason: Ill-typed against general CRRA form for gamma != 1 (log-returns can be negative; raising negative numbers to fractional powers produces complex values).

rejected_candidates.name: terminal_wealth_W = prod(1 + r_i)
rejected_candidates.reason: Collapses T -> 1; binding condition S-2 requires t-stat = mean(U)/(sd(U)/sqrt(T)) with T>=2; sd(U) undefined for T=1.

worked_examples_metadata.input_unit_note: The 'inputs' blocks below carry guard_alpha, eod_return, r_policy in DECIMAL-FRACTION units. A +5% day appears as r_policy=0.05, not r_policy=5.0. To reconstruct from codebase: codebase_value_pct / RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION = fixture_value_fraction.
worked_examples_metadata.test_harness_responsibility: The M1 RED test (tests/autotuner/test_m1_wealth_argument_derivation.py) must either (a) construct synthetic tick streams with percent-frame returns that, post-conversion, match these inputs, or (b) call derive_wealth_argument directly with decimal-fraction inputs and a docstring contract pinning the input frame.

worked_examples.scenario: Typical small gain day; well above floor
Typical small loss day; well above floor
Moderate gain day; well above floor
Large but realistic loss day (-30%); still above floor; tests monotonicity and finiteness in the regime where CRRA risk-aversion matters most.
Floor-hitting day (catastrophic synthetic case): r_policy = -0.9999 implies W_raw=0.0001 < WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR=0.001; floor applied; U finite for all prudential gamma.
worked_examples.discriminating_power_note: Tests the W-H4 contract: floor applied to W (not U); U is finite; sd(U) over a series containing this day reflects the honest monotone CRRA shape of u(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR), not a U-side compressed-tail artifact.

U_naive_without_floor_for_comparison.note: Without the floor, gamma=2.0 case would yield U = 1 - 1/0.0001 = -9999.0 (still finite for W=1e-4 but consuming an extra order of magnitude of IEEE-754 budget); had W reached 0 exactly, U -> -inf for gamma>=1 -- the binding H-1 NaN-poisoning failure mode.

A4_consistency_assertion.claim: Wherever CRRA is evaluated in Phase-1 M1, W = max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, 1 + r_policy_fraction). Same definition; same units (decimal-fraction); same horizon (one trading day close-to-close). Unit conversion from percent (codebase native) to decimal-fraction (CRRA-formula domain) happens once at the autotuner branch boundary via RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION = 100.0.
phase_1_call_sites: autotuner.py _collect_sim_returns -> emits per-day triggered_return series in PERCENT frame (lines 672-695)
autotuner.py run_simulation -> emits per-day triggered_return series in PERCENT frame (lines 752-777)
autotuner branch boundary -> divide by RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION = 100.0 (decimal-fraction frame)
compute_crra_eu_objective(daily_returns_fraction, gamma) -> consumes decimal-fraction; computes mean(U) where U_i = u(max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, 1 + r_i_fraction); gamma)
compute_crra_eu_tstat(U_series) -> S-2 t-stat over the same U_series
A4_consistency_assertion.phase_2_extension_note: If Finalist B unlocks a hold-vs-exit CRRA crossover, the hold-branch W uses the SAME definitional form W = max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, 1 + r_policy_fraction) evaluated on the hypothetical hold path. The form is the persistent NN1 facet; only the data source (realized vs. simulated) changes. Phase-2 plumbing is out-of-scope here.

test_assertions_supported: RED-test-2 Scenario 1: element-wise W matches per-formula computation (use examples ex1-ex4 where floor not applied; tolerance per U_by_gamma.tolerance_for_test).
RED-test-2 Scenario 2: ex5 demonstrates W_floored == WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR exactly (not approx); other examples demonstrate W_floored == W_raw exactly when floor not applied.

RED-test-2 Scenario 3 (discriminating): ex5 supports asserting sd(U) computed with W-side floor differs from a hypothetical U-side floor by a closed-form known gap; see U_naive_without_floor_for_comparison for the gamma=2.0 contrast.
RED-test-2 Scenario 4: ex5's U_by_gamma values are all finite (math.isfinite true) and the implied t-stat over a series containing this day is finite.
RED-test-2 Scenario 5: ex1-ex4 demonstrate that derive_floored_wealth_argument == derive_wealth_argument when no day breaches the floor.
limitations: Worked U values computed at IEEE-754 double precision; test tolerances are set conservatively above the round-off floor.
Floor at 1e-3 is sized for prudential gamma <= 5; revisit if the operator pre-registers gamma > 10.
Serial-correlation residual (W-H5 / H-6) is disclose-and-accept per M1 plan; the t-stat finiteness assertion does NOT claim t-stat unbiasedness in the presence of lag-1 autocorrelation.

W-H4-B (information-collapse on floor-hit trials): for gamma >= 2, a single floor-hit day's |U| overwhelms the other ~24 fold-day utilities; the per-trial t-stat pins at sign(U_floor) * sqrt((T-1)/T) ~ -1 regardless of non-floor-day performance, so any floor-hit trial is effectively dropped by BHY (p_adj ~ 1). This is treated as correct policy behavior (a catastrophic single-day loss SHOULD disqualify the parameter set) and is disclose-and-accept per M1 plan; flagged in the autotuner docstring. No Phase-1 remediation.

fixture_schema_check.rule_D2_non_circular_provenance: Memo IS the producer (the W-H2 derivation document); fixture is schema-derived from that memo; this fixture is NOT authored alongside the SUT.
fixture_schema_check.rule_no_magic_numbers: Every constant (WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, CRRA_LOG_UTILITY_GAMMA_TOL_suggestion, gamma values) is named and provenanced.
